hero.title: Industry Trends, Practical Experience, and Integration Guides
hero.description: Focusing on verification code reception, project integration, risk control protection, and channel operations. Continuously outputting content suitable for developers and business teams.

items.title: How to Improve the Success Rate of Receiving Codes
items.description: Improve overall reception performance from the dimensions of country, project, channel scheduling, and verification code polling strategies.

items.title: How to Design a More Secure Dual Token Mechanism
items.description: Reduce the risk of business interruption caused by authentication failure through the cooperation of token and refreshToken.

items.title: Why Data Transparency is Important for Receiving Platforms
items.description: Explain the value of transparent data capabilities from the perspectives of order tracking, status backtracking, and anomaly troubleshooting.

hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions & Quick Support
hero.description: Summarizes high-frequency problems in business integration, authentication, number acquisition, verification code polling, and anomaly troubleshooting to help you quickly locate and solve issues.

items.question: Why can't I get a number?
items.answer: Please first check your balance, project status, whether the country project is available, and whether the current IP is in the whitelist.

items.question: Why hasn't the verification code been returned?
items.answer: Please confirm whether the number status has entered the waiting for verification code stage, and regularly poll for the verification code according to the interface requirements. Enable callbackUrl if necessary.

items.question: What should I do if the Token expires?
items.answer: Please use refreshToken to refresh and get a new token; if the refreshToken also expires, you need to re-authenticate via the authentication interface.

items.question: Why is the interface intercepted?
items.answer: In most cases, it is caused by the IP whitelist not being configured, authentication information errors, or triggered security firewall policies.

hero.title: Focusing on Global Number Receiving & Verification Code Integration Services
hero.description: We are committed to building a more stable, transparent, and secure code-receiving capability platform, providing reliable SMS verification code reception services for developers, studios, and enterprise teams.

seo.title: About Us
seo.description: Learn about our platform philosophy, service capabilities, and contact information.

items.title: Stability First
items.description: Through multi-channel scheduling and resource integration, we provide continuously available number and verification code services for your business.

items.title: Transparent & Trustworthy
items.description: Adhering to price transparency, status transparency, and data transparency, ensuring every API call is traceable.

items.title: Secure & Controllable
items.description: Ensuring the security of platform and customer data through authentication, refresh mechanisms, firewalls, and whitelist policies.

contacts.title: Contact Us
contacts.description: If you need channel support, business cooperation, API integration help, or technical assistance, you can contact the team through the following methods.

openApiPage.title: OpenAPI Configuration
openApiPage.description: Manage your API switches, whitelist IPs, APPID, and secret key information, and view integration and technical support guidelines.

status.hint: If your account has not yet enabled API, you can actively activate it here.

whiteIp.label: Whitelist IP
whiteIp.placeholder: Please enter whitelist IPs, separating multiple IPs with English commas
whiteIp.helper: It is recommended to fill in the server's egress IP. Unconfigured IPs may be intercepted by security policies.

items: Before integrating, please confirm you have obtained the APPID, API Key, and configured the whitelist IP.
It is recommended to uniformly use the JSON request format for global interfaces.
Please securely save the token and refreshToken for login state-related interfaces.
If you encounter integration issues, please provide the request and response messages for easier troubleshooting.
If receiving the verification code is abnormal, please check the project, country, and callback address configurations.
If you need channel support or business cooperation, please contact the team via the contact info on the About Us page.

hero.title: Business Dashboard Overview
hero.description: Centrally view account status, OpenAPI activation, financial overview, and system support information. This can later be expanded into a real-time order, channel, verification code, and revenue cockpit.

overview.availableBalanceDesc: The account balance currently available directly for placing orders, fetching numbers, and business consumption.
overview.todayAmountDesc: The cumulative consumption amount based on today's orders, suitable for quickly understanding fund usage.
overview.freezeBalanceDesc: Funds temporarily frozen during order processing or the pending settlement phase.
overview.totalBalanceDesc: Total Account Balance = Available Balance + Frozen Balance.
overview.successRateDesc: The overall success rate based on today's orders, used to judge current business quality.
overview.waitingCodeCountDesc: The number of phones currently still waiting for a verification code, helping users judge processing timeliness.
overview.openApiEnabledDesc: The current account has enabled API capabilities and can perform direct server integration.
overview.openApiDisabledDesc: The current account has not yet enabled API capabilities. We recommend configuring it on the OpenAPI page.

account.title: Account & Credentials
account.description: Displays the basic identity, interface credentials, and integration status of the currently logged-in account.

business.title: Business Cockpit
business.description: The structure here is temporarily built based on the current project's business model. The backend only needs to return real data according to the fields to replace placeholder content.

sections.title: Orders Overview
sections.description: It is recommended to integrate today's total orders, successes, failures, success rate, and total consumption amount, and support a 7-day trend.

sections.title: Number Business
sections.description: It is recommended to integrate the current number fetching count, waiting code count, released count, blacklisted count, and distribution across countries/projects.

sections.title: Channel Status
sections.description: It is recommended to integrate channel availability rates, number of countries supported by projects, number of abnormal channels, average code retrieval time, and other metrics.

sections.title: Security & Integration
sections.description: It is recommended to integrate OpenAPI request volume, whitelist hits, interceptions, recent API exceptions, and other data.

balanceRatio.title: Balance Ratio
balanceRatio.description: Displays the structural proportion of available balance and frozen balance, helping users quickly understand their fund status.

orderRatio.title: Order Status Ratio
orderRatio.description: Displays the distribution of today's successful, failed, and processing orders as a proportion.

projectRatio.title: Project Ratio
projectRatio.description: Displays the order or consumption proportion of major projects, making it easier for users to identify their core business.

countryRatio.title: Country Ratio
countryRatio.description: Displays the business proportion and success rate performance of major countries.

trend.title: 7-Day Trend
trend.description: It is recommended for the backend to return trends in order volume, success rate, and consumption amount for graphical representation of business changes.

backend.title: Suggested Backend Interface Structure
backend.description: It is recommended to add a summary interface for the console, such as /admin/dashboard/summary, returning data according to the following fields.

fields.name: balance.total
fields.desc: Total Balance, can be directly returned by the backend

fields.name: orders.successRate
fields.desc: Success Rate, suggested to return a percentage value or a formatable number

fields.name: orders.processingCount
fields.desc: Number of Processing Orders, used for the order status proportion chart

fields.name: numbers.inUseCount
fields.desc: Number of phones currently in use

fields.name: numbers.waitingCodeCount
fields.desc: Number of phones waiting for a verification code

fields.name: trends.sevenDays
fields.desc: 7-day trend array, each item may contain date, orderCount, successCount, amount

fields.name: projects.distribution
fields.desc: Project distribution array, each item suggested to contain name, value, amount, successRate, color

fields.name: countries.distribution
fields.desc: Country distribution array, each item suggested to contain name, value, successRate, color

hero.title: A Transparent, Stable, and High-Success Rate SMS Platform
hero.description: Focusing on verification code reception and number services, providing transparent pricing, transparent data, security protection, rich channels, and high-success-rate global SMS capabilities to help you complete your business verification processes more efficiently.

items.title: Transparent Pricing
items.description: Project prices are clearly visible, with no hidden fees. Operations like obtaining, releasing, and blacklisting numbers are straightforward.

items.title: Data Transparency
items.description: Order status, code retrieval, and number usage are fully traceable across the entire process, facilitating auditing and troubleshooting.

items.title: Security Protection
items.description: Supports Token authentication, refresh mechanisms, and security firewall interception to protect API access and business data.

items.title: Rich Channels
items.description: Multi-country, multi-project, and multi-channel supply flexibly adapting to different business scenarios and verification needs.

items.title: High Success Rate
items.description: Stable number resources and multi-channel scheduling strategies effectively improve verification code reception success rates and business efficiency.

items.title: Flexible Callbacks
items.description: Supports both active polling and callback push modes, making it convenient to integrate into automated business workflows.

hero.title: API Documentation
hero.description: Provides universal API documentation to help developers quickly integrate with this platform and enjoy efficient and stable services.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.lead: Before using the project interfaces, please ensure you meet the following conditions:
items: Possess basic knowledge of network requests 😪
Have developer capabilities 😨
Be familiar with the dual Token refresh mechanism 😥
Possess the ability to debug, query logs, and troubleshoot problems 😁
introduction.summary: This project aims to provide a universal API integration solution to help you quickly realize business function integration.
introduction.summaryTitle: This project should be a Universal API
summaryItems: There is no fixed request address; the request address depends on your account address.
Usually, the request address is the currently used IP / Domain.
Please strictly follow the documentation requirements for development.
If you need technical support, please describe the problem, the bug reproduction process, and provide the request and response messages.
introduction.note: The documentation is written quite casually, please go easy on me 💔

authentication.title: Integration Prerequisites & Authentication
authentication.lead: Before integration, you should obtain your APPID and apiKey token from the dashboard backend:
items: appid: The currently used Merchant / User ID.
apikey: A system-specific encrypted key generated for interface signatures or direct authentication.
IP Whitelist: Must be configured as your server's outbound request IP; if not configured, you will be directly intercepted by the firewall.
When configuring multiple IPs, please use English commas to separate them.

apiStandards.title: Universal API Standards
items: Request Address: There is no fixed request address; the request address depends on your assigned account address (usually the currently used IP / Domain).
Request Method: The POST method is used globally; GET methods are not accepted (unless otherwise specified in the documentation).
Request Type: The request header Content-Type must strictly be specified as application/json.
Naming Convention: Most data return fields use camelCase naming; if not, it will be specifically noted in the specific interface documentation.

responseFormat.title: Universal Response Format
responseFormat.lead: All interfaces return standard JSON format data. Determine whether the business processing is successful based on the 'code' status code.
responseFormat.note: Note: When the code is not 200/0, it usually means a business or system exception has occurred. Please refer to the 'msg' field for troubleshooting.

apiList.title: API Interface List
apiList.lead: Below are the currently open core interfaces. It is recommended to integrate them in calling order.

items.title: Get Token
items.summary: For any operations within the API, you must obtain a token and refreshToken via this interface. This interface supports POST/GET parameter passing and form-data/json inputs; it is recommended to uniformly use json.

params.name: appKey
params.desc: The apiKey generated by the platform

items.title: Refresh Token
items.summary: This interface supports GET/POST requests and takes the parameter refreshToken. It supports form-data/json. Upon successful refresh, you will receive a new token and a new refreshToken.

params.name: refreshToken
params.desc: The refresh token saved after login

items.title: Get Account Information
items.summary: Obtains the user's account information. If needed, pass Authorization in the request header; the token can be accompanied by the Bearer {Token} prefix.

params.name: Authorization
params.desc: The token obtained after user login, supports Bearer prefix

items.successExample:
{
  "code": 200,
  "msg": "User info obtained successfully",
  "data": {
    "userName": "1008611",
    "gender": 0,
    "phone": null,
    "email": null,
    "avatar": "https://hk10000.cn/make/assets/images/avatar.jpg",
    "balance": 197,
    "lastLoginTime": "2026-01-15 06:55:09",
    "lastLoginIp": "172.17.0.1",
    "lastLoginDevice": "Chrome 143.0.0 / Windows 10.0.0"
  },
  "time": 1768431333
}

items.title: Country & Region Library
items.summary: Gets the address information library within the system. The country library generally does not change much, so it is recommended to store it locally.

items.title: Get Mobile Number
items.summary: Get a mobile number; supports form-data/json. After obtaining the number, the status will change to waiting for verification code. At this time, you need to call the get verification code interface. Newly added return field orderNo, which needs to be passed when getting the verification code.

params.name: countryId
params.desc: Country ID, corresponding to the country field in projects

params.name: mobile
params.desc: Can be passed if you want to continue using the previous number

params.name: type
params.desc: Number type, options are sms / voice / incall. Can also be 'all' or empty

items.title: Get Verification Code
items.summary: Supports form-data/json. This interface requires regular polling to get the verification code. After returning code=200, the code will not be returned repeatedly subsequently.

params.name: orderNo
params.desc: The unique order number returned by the get number interface

params.name: phone
params.desc: The mobile number returned by the get number interface

params.name: pkey
params.desc: The pkey returned by the get number interface

items.title: Release Number
items.summary: Supports form-data/json. The interface consumer can actively call the release interface to avoid extra charges.

items.title: Blacklist Number
items.summary: Supports form-data/json. The interface consumer can call this to blacklist a number, and that number will not be retrieved subsequently.

items.title: SMS Callback Push
items.summary: Pass the callbackUrl when fetching a number. The system will actively push to this address when an SMS from operators is received later.
